title: FAQ Classifier
prompt_md:
## Goal
Write a program that reads C++ FAQ topic codes and prints the matching category name.

## Input
- Line 1: integer N (1 ≤ N ≤ 20).
- Lines 2..N+1: one uppercase topic code per line. Valid codes and their categories:
  - `UB` → `undefined behavior`
  - `RAII` → `resource management`
  - `HEADERS` → `build system`
  - `NAMESPACE` → `naming`
  - `SMART` → `resource management`

## Output
For each code, print the category on its own line.

## Requirements
1. All five codes must map correctly.
2. Note that both `RAII` and `SMART` map to `resource management`.
3. One result per line, no trailing spaces.

test_cases.label: Sample: all five codes
test_cases.expected_stdout:
undefined behavior
resource management
build system
naming
resource management

title: Include Guard Checker
prompt_md:
## Goal
Complete the function `hasIncludeGuard` that checks whether a simplified header file representation includes a proper include guard.

## Input
- Line 1: integer N (1 ≤ N ≤ 20), number of header file descriptions.
- For each header: two lines:
  - Line 1: `yes` or `no` indicating whether `#pragma once` is present at the top.
  - Line 2: `yes` or `no` indicating whether a matching `#ifndef`/`#define`/`#endif` guard is present.

## Output
For each header, print `guarded` if either (or both) protection mechanisms are present, otherwise print `unguarded`.

## Requirements
1. A header is `guarded` if `pragma_once` is `yes` OR `ifndef_guard` is `yes` (or both).
2. A header is `unguarded` only if both are `no`.
3. One result per line.

test_cases.label: Sample: three headers
test_cases.stdin:
3
yes
no
no
yes
no
no
